tagline: XML-structured, context-before-task, effort scaled to complexity.
effort_line: Run at high/xhigh effort and reason adaptively — don't pad. Scale tool/step effort to task size with a number, not 'be thorough'. End with one sharp directive.
idioms: Put context before the task; give one non-overlapping rule per section.
Structure precedence-sensitive logic as a numbered checklist with an explicit stop rule.
notes: Anthropic's flagship: a calibrated generalist whose strength is judgment. Prompt it like its own system prompt is built — context before task, one rule per section, numbered checklists with explicit stop/verify conditions, and a numeric effort budget scaled to task size. Let it infer and state assumptions rather than gating on questions; keep the prompt concise and end with a single capstone directive. Trust adaptive reasoning; name any wanted parallelism or verification explicitly.
